title: antennaria friesiana subsp. alaskana
intro: Antennaria friesiana subsp. alaskana, also known as Alaskan Yarrow or Mountain Yarrow, is a plant species native to Alaska and western Canada, belonging to the Asteraceae family and commonly used in traditional medicine. This plant has been found to possess medicinal properties due to its saponin and flavonoid content, which contribute to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Antennaria friesiana subsp. alaskana include its ability to alleviate digestive issues, reduce fever, and support immune function, making it a valuable remedy for various ailments. The plant is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, which can be used topically to soothe skin irritations and wounds, or internally to promote overall well-being.
